# Introduction

**The PG Campaign Spark Book ** 

**High-level ideas and creative prompts meant to inspire, challenge,**   
**and elevate every launch ahead.**

**NOTE:** THE IDEAS IN THIS DOCUMENT **ARE NOT FINAL**; JUST THOUGHT STARTERS.

At Performance Golf, everything we build moves in one direction: toward helping everyday golfers play better, faster — and toward a world where progress feels personal, magical, and unmistakably real. Over the next nine months, we’ll be launching some of the most ambitious products, experiences, and innovations in our company’s history. Each launch is more than a release on a calendar; it’s a chance to tell a story, deepen belief in our brand, and create breakthroughs for golfers who are relying on us to guide their improvement.

The purpose of this document is to share my early thinking for each launch — the themes I’m feeling, the angles that excite me, and the sparks I believe could ignite a campaign. These are not final directions, locked decisions, or rigid templates. They’re the first brushstrokes. Thought starters. Raw concepts meant to open the door, not close it.

Use these ideas as fuel. Build on them. Challenge them. Break them apart and reshape them into something bigger, bolder, and more cinematic. The brand we’re building demands creativity that is both strategic and expansive — a blend of emotional storytelling and measurable clarity that inspires golfers to take the next step on their path.

As you read through each launch concept, ask yourself:

* *How do we make this idea feel even more transformative?*  
* *How do we bring this to life visually in a way only Performance Golf would?*  
* *Where are the magic moments — and how do we amplify them?*  
* *What’s the unexpected twist that turns this from a product launch into a brand moment?*

Our mission remains the same: create breakthrough work that lifts the brand, differentiates us in the market, and gives golfers a reason to believe in a smarter, simpler path to their best game. This document is simply the beginning. The real magic happens when each of you brings your craft, your perspective, and your ambition to these ideas — and together, we turn possibility into reality.

# Founder's PG1 Launch

**PG1 Founder’s Launch** 

**This is the year your game moves forward.**

PG1 is more than an app — it’s the beginning of a new era in golf improvement. For decades, everyday golfers have been stuck inside a broken system: random tips, endless videos, conflicting advice, and practice sessions built on guesswork instead of understanding. The result? Players spend more time chasing fixes than making progress. They get older but not better. They love golf deeply, yet feel increasingly frustrated by a game that seems unwilling to reward their effort. The PG1 App exists to change all of that. It’s the world’s first and only *personalized game improvement system* — a smart, adaptive, human-driven coaching platform that brings data, AI, world-class instruction, and purpose-built product accelerators together into one clear path forward. For the first time, golfers have a guide that knows their game, shapes their practice, and pulls them out of confusion and back into momentum.

This launch is anchored in Brixton’s founding story — the relentless journey of someone who refused to accept the idea that improvement should take 5–10 years. His obsession has always been simple: *play better, faster.* He turned over every stone, studied with leading coaches, tested every method, and explored every technology until he discovered patterns that most golfers will never see on their own. PG1 is the system that emerged from that pursuit. This Founder’s Launch isn’t just a product release — it’s an invitation into the “why” behind Performance Golf: a promise that there is a smarter, simpler, more joyful way to get better at the game you love.

The digital landscape is a perfect setup for this story. Every minute, the internet floods golfers with more noise — new tips, new drills, new “fixes.” We can visualize this in the campaign through a **live stock-ticker style counter** that scrolls nonstop: **“XX new golf instructional videos uploaded … XX new minutes of content added…”** The numbers climb by the second, symbolizing the avalanche of advice that overwhelms players and actively makes improvement harder. Against that chaos, PG1 stands as the opposite: clarity. A single path. The right step at the right time in the right order. This contrast — *chaos vs. clarity* — can become one of the most memorable emotional anchors of the entire campaign.

Because of this, the launch becomes an opportunity to make that clarity tangible. A **Founder’s Launch Giveaway** reinforces the narrative: ten golfers receive lifetime access to PG1, three receive VIP Golf School experiences, and winners receive club and gear packages — a gesture that signals belief, generosity, and commitment. The **Founder’s Promise Guarantee** pushes even further: shave 5 shots off your score in 90 days if you follow the system. It’s bold, emotional, and aligned with the founding story. It also gives golfers something they’ve never been offered before — a measurable, time-bound commitment that connects improvement to accountability and removes the fear that “this will be just another thing that won’t work.”

The stories that support this launch must feel human, personal, and deeply relatable. Three players. Three journeys. Three rounds on the course with Brixton — sharing their frustrations, their setbacks, their failed attempts, and the moment PG1 finally made the path feel clear. Shot in the wild during real golf, these pieces become living proof of what structured guidance and personalized instruction can do. This is where we shift from “product demo” to “emotional connection.” Because golfers don’t just want tips — they want *hope*. They want a reason to believe that their best golf is still ahead of them.

But the emotional side is only half of the story. This is also a chance to highlight the *innovation* behind PG1. The team should think Apple-level storytelling: clean animation, beautiful UI, elegant systems thinking, 3D visualizations of data inputs, AI swing diagnostics, coach messaging, and practice plans updating in real time. This is not “another golf app.” It’s an ecosystem. It’s coaching meets technology meets human connection.

An ROI/time-savings calculator could add an additional dimension. It reframes the value of starting now vs. later. It shows golfers the cost of procrastination — not in dollars, but in years of progress lost. If they start today, our math shows them exactly how many shots and how many months they will save. If they wait 1, 5, or 10 years, they see how far behind they’ll fall. It’s simple, emotional, and behavioral science at its finest. It tells the golfer: “You’ve waited long enough. Your path starts now.”

This Founder’s Launch is ultimately about one idea: *Progress becomes possible the moment confusion ends.* PG1 ends the confusion. It aligns with our brand’s promise, our archetype, and our mission. And this launch gives us the perfect first chapter — a soft launch that reignites current users, introduces new ones, and helps us test messaging before March’s full-scale “Love Your Game” brand rollout. Emotional storytelling meets cutting-edge innovation. Human truth meets system-level clarity. This is how we ignite belief before we ignite the world.

**Cool video from Rob Dyrdek on his time-operating platform he built. Could be a cool idea for how Brixton can talk to PG1 with the visuals in the background. [JoJo Phillips](mailto:jojo@performancegolfzone.com)**[https://www.instagram.com/reel/DRAS2zkjyUH/?igsh=MXUwY28xMjd3cGM4Yw==](https://www.instagram.com/reel/DRAS2zkjyUH/?igsh=MXUwY28xMjd3cGM4Yw==)

### **Questions to Prompt Ideas:**

### **1\. The Founder’s Story**

* How do we visually dramatize Brixton’s journey from frustration → obsession → breakthrough → system creation?  
* What emotional beats must we capture to make golfers feel seen, understood, and inspired?

### **2\. The Giveaway \+ Founder’s Promise Guarantee**

* How do we frame the Founder's Promise Guarantee so it feels bold but credible — rooted in accountability, not hype?  
* How do we elevate the Lifetime Membership and VIP School giveaways so they feel like entering a movement, not a sweepstakes?

### **3\. The Human Stories**

* What moments during a round with Brixton create the most real, emotional storytelling?  
* Which pain points (trying YouTube, taking random lessons, years of practicing the wrong things) can we surface authentically?

### **4\. The Chaos vs. Clarity Message**

* How might we illustrate the overwhelming chaos of modern golf content in a cinematic, graphic, or humorous way?  
* What visual metaphors best depict “lessons,” “tips,” and “advice” piling up faster than golfers can process?

### **5\. The Calculator / Time-Savings Angle**

* How do we turn the calculator into a hero feature that shoppers *play with,* not just skim?  
* What emotional insight can we anchor it to? (“You’ve lost enough time. Don’t lose more.”)

### **6\. Showcasing the Innovation**

* How do we make the app feel like the most advanced, intuitive, premium tool in golf?  
* What animation style, motion language, and UI focus makes this feel like the “Apple of golf improvement”?

### **7\. System Thinking**

* How do we position PG1 as a system — not an app, not a lesson provider, but *the* pathway?  
* Where can we integrate messaging like: “The right step. The right time. The right order.”?

# SpeedTrac

**SpeedTrac** 

**Outdrive Yesterday.**

SpeedTrac is more than a swing speed trainer — it’s a comeback machine for the everyday golfer. It’s the first training aid that ***doesn’t just help you swing faster; it helps you swing better.*** With its adjustable weighting system and movable positions inside the track, golfers can finally train for **both raw speed and face control** in a single tool. The result is a training experience that makes players feel like they’re stepping back into a younger, more confident version of themselves — the golfer who swung freely, hit it past their buddies, and believed every tee shot had a chance to be their longest of the day.

For many golfers, the first thing age takes away is **speed**. Not passion. Not effort. Speed. That slow, quiet decline in clubhead velocity often feels like a loss of identity — distance disappears, ego takes a hit, and suddenly the game feels harder than it should. SpeedTrac is built to reverse that decline. Not with gimmicks or unrealistic promises, but with a system rooted in science, sequencing, and progressive overload. The adjustable weights allow golfers to move up and down a “speed ladder,” training their body to produce more power with control — and generating measurable results they can feel instantly.

The design itself opens a creative door that’s rare in golf. Yes, its silhouette playfully nods to the **flux capacitor**, and yes, Chris McGinley — our own mad-scientist innovator — created it in a moment of pure inspiration. But the bigger story isn’t time travel; it’s transformation. It’s the idea that golfers can reclaim what they thought was gone. That they can turn back the clock on their swing speed and unlock a level of power they assumed was behind them. SpeedTrac makes that feeling real — because the gains aren’t theoretical. They happen in front of you, on camera, on radar, in real time.

And that’s where the magic happens: SpeedTrac isn’t just felt — it’s measured. One 60-second cycle can add 3–5 mph of speed. One session can produce straighter shots through better face rotation. This creates the perfect storm for social proof: range challenges, simulator battles, Top Golf “back wall” attempts, old golfers outdriving young bucks, skinny guys nuking it past their “athlete” friends. It taps into competition, pride, the joy of surprising yourself, and the thrill of feeling something powerful and new. Losing distance is one of the most demoralizing experiences in golf. SpeedTrac brings that distance back — replacing frustration with momentum, confidence, and rediscovered joy.

[**Link to PG Research**](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1WZvq6mSzPKBb_6aFgBNpMhVb0vwmLqP7U_fJVNbtXIw/edit?usp=sharing)

### **Major Pillars:**

* The emotional story of losing speed  
* The innovation \+ physics behind the design  
* The dual-benefit (speed \+ face control)  
* Social proof opportunities  
* Ego dynamics  
* Ladder-style training  
* Performance Golf’s system-driven transformation

### **![][image1]**

### **Questions to Prompt Ideas:** 

### **1\. Emotional Angles**

* How do we dramatize the emotional pain of losing distance in a way that feels relatable, humorous, or cathartic — not shame-based?  
* What moments in golf (the tee box with buddies, long drive contests, hitting the back net at TopGolf) best capture the joy of increased speed?

### **2\. “Back to the Future” World-Building**

* How far should we lean into the flux capacitor / time machine metaphor?  
  Light touch? Full comedic sketch? Cinematic parody?  
* Can Chris play the “Doc Brown” archetype in a way that feels iconic, not cheesy?  
* What’s the visual language of “reversing time” or “rewinding your swing”?

### **3\. Data & Demonstration**

* How do we film and package the “increase your speed by 5 mph in 60 seconds” challenge in a way that’s social-first, repeatable, and viral?  
* What environments (range, sim, home, Top Golf) give us the most compelling before/after content?

### **4\. The Unique Tech Story**

* How do we clearly show that SpeedTrac is the **first** speed trainer that also trains **face control** through movable weight positions?  
* What visual metaphors (rails, tracks, sliders, energy beams, compression moments) help us tell this story instantly?

### **5\. The Relatability Factor**

* How do we represent the “older golfer getting longer again” in a way that feels empowering rather than cliché?  
* What archetypal moments (beating your buddy, carrying the bunker, driving the green, reaching a par 5 in two) communicate regained vitality?

### **6\. Caveman Instinct / Ego Dynamics**

* How do we harness male competitiveness — “outdrive your buddies,” “beat the athlete,” “shock your group” — while keeping it fun, modern, and on-brand?  
* What’s the clever, memorable, PG-style way to show “the ego boost”?

### **7\. The System Layer**

* How do we position the progressive, ladder-style training program as a **system** rather than just a stick with weights?  
* How can we ensure the SpeedTrac is positioned within the larger Performance Golf ecosystem and path to progress in the story? Leading to users adding more to their basket or higher LTV?

**Campaign / Activation Idea** from [Brixton Albert](mailto:brixton@performancegolfzone.com)

* Use AI app \- tracks your swing speed, tie in speed trac training aid  
* create mega social buzz \- AI app tracks swing speed  
* challenge to gain the most speed wins X prize  
* launch product and have this around it to create 360 degree buzz

**The Golf Speed Academy** (Master Speed in 30 or 60 Days)  
**Overhead:** Almost zero

**Why this works:**  
Speed is the single easiest thing to measure and market.

**Offer structure:**

* Daily overspeed drills  
* Personalized AI speed roadmap  
* Weekly benchmarks  
* Swing upload for speed feedback  
* Leaderboard \+ speed badge system

**Why it will sell:**  
Speed is sexy. It's measurable. People can brag about mph.  
And even \+3 mph feels like a transformation.

# ONE.1 \+ ONE.S Wedge

**ONE.1 & ONE.S** 

**The Wedge Built for the Lies That Break Most Golfers.**

Around the greens is where golfers feel the most exposed. Full swings are one thing; you can hide behind a decent drive. But a tight lie over a bunker, buried ball in the lip, or gnarly rough around the collar? That’s short-game hell. That’s where chunked chips, skulled rockets, and three-putt doubles live. And nothing hits the ego harder than butchering a simple pitch in front of your friends. The new ONE.1 and ONE.S Wedges are built to be the **legal cheat code** for those moments — the club you pull when you absolutely refuse to be the punchline.

This isn’t just a quiet iteration on a best-seller. The ONE Wedge is a statement: *“We’re done pretending the average golfer should use the same wedges built for tour robots.”* The re-engineered sole, updated weighting, and forgiveness-focused design are all about one promise: **one wedge, one swing, all the nasty lies.** Bunkers, hardpan, thick rough, tight fairway… if the lie looks like a horror show, this is the wedge you trust. That opens the door to a campaign world that doesn’t play it safe. We can drop golfers into the most ridiculous, intimidating, “no chance” situations we can find and let ONE do the talking.

Instead of a polite product launch, we treat this like a **short-game revolt**. Custom wedges sent to creators in over-the-top “Short Game From Hell” kits. Challenge videos from the world’s most brutal bunkers and ridiculous lies. Head-to-head “Wedge Wars” against the biggest names in golf — blind tests where regular golfers don’t know which wedge is which, they just know which one got them up and down. UGC contests where golfers submit their worst lies and we crown the “Most Ridiculous Up & Down” of the season. The ONE Wedge becomes less of a club and more of a dare: *“Name the lie. We’ll take that shot.”* That’s the kind of energy that gets people talking, sharing, and rethinking what “game improvement” gear can look like.

And under all the fun, there’s still the Performance Golf system story: the wedge is an accelerator that plugs into PG1 and our coaching philosophy. It’s not just about the club — it’s about giving everyday golfers a weapon that makes the hardest part of the game feel less random and more inevitable. When they pull the ONE, they’re not hoping. They’re expecting. 

## **CAMPAIGN PILLARS & BIG IDEA CONCEPTS**

1. **Short Game From Hell Tour**  
   * Take ONE Wedge to the **most brutal lies and bunkers** you can find: cavernous links bunkers, railroad-tie edges, insane pot bunkers, vertical faces, desert waste areas, hardpan parking-lot lies.  
   * Film coaches, influencers, and everyday golfers with one rule: *you only get the ONE Wedge.*  
   * Tagline territory: **“If it works here, it works anywhere.”**

2. **Wedge Wars: ONE vs Everyone**  
   * Blind test against Vokey, Callaway, TaylorMade, etc.  
   * Regular golfers don’t know which is which; they just react: contact, launch, confidence, up-and-down success.  
   * Turn it into a recurring content series: **“ONE vs The Field.”**  
   * Data overlays: % chunks, % skulls, distance to hole, up-and-down conversion.

3. **Influencer “Short Game Hell Kit” Seeding**  
   * Custom ONE Wedge boxes themed like an emergency survival kit:  
     * “SHORT GAME HELL KIT”  
     * Includes: ONE Wedge, a “You Are Not the Problem, Your Wedge Is” note from PG, and other relevant items.  
   * Encourage creators to unbox, then immediately take it to their **worst local bunker** or lie and film first impressions.

4. **The “Worst Lie Wins” Challenge**  
   * UGC campaign: golfers submit videos of their **most impossible lies** conquered with the ONE Wedge.  
   * Winner gets a trip to a PG VIP experience \+ a full short-game fitting / coaching day.  
   * Could ladder into an annual “Short Game From Hell” highlight reel.

5. **ONE Ball, ONE Wedge, ONE Chance**  
   * Content format: golfers get **one ball, one wedge, one shot** from a disgusting lie.  
   * If they pull it off → instant up-and-down, they “escape.” If they don’t → comedic penalty (ice bath, hot sauce, bag carry, etc.).  
   * Perfect for Reels/TikTok — quick, watchable tension.  
   * Could be good for a Charity Golf Scramble sponsorship

6. **Short Game Therapy — Stop the Chunks**  
   * Slightly more emotional spin: real golfers describing the embarrassment of blading/chunking around the green.  
   * Then: we put ONE Wedge 2.0 in their hands, film the “first time I felt like I could actually pull this off” moments.  
   * Tagline angle: **“You’re not bad at golf. Your wedge just hasn’t been on your side.”**

7. **ONE Wedge x PG1 Missions**  
   * In the app, create **“ONE Wedge Missions”**: short-game challenges, drills, and games built around the wedge’s strengths.  
   * Tie real-world product to digital system: app shows you what to practice, wedge gives you forgiveness to execute.  
   * Outcome: product \+ software synergy — this isn’t just a wedge, it’s part of your guided path.

8. **The 10 Lies Challenge (Brand Content, Not Legal Guarantee… Yet)**  
   * Define the 10 nastiest lies an everyday golfer faces (tight lie, wet rough, deep bunker, downhill chip, etc.).  
   * Challenge creators: **“If ONE Wedge doesn’t beat your current wedge on at least 7/10 of these… roast us on camera.”**  
   * Confidence flex \+ transparency \+ content gold.

9. **Short Game Horror Film → Short Game Redemption Film**  
   * Cinematic brand spot:  
     * Open with nightmare montage — chunks, blades, lip-outs, the “walk of shame” from the fringe.  
     * Then we cut to ONE Wedge: same lies, different results.  
   * Tone: part tongue-in-cheek horror trailer, part redemption arc.  
   * Ends with a dead-simple message: **“ONE Wedge. Same golfer. Different outcome.”**
